summary: veteran postinstall fetches and executes a binary from a non-publisher domain
details: On `npm install`, the package's `postinstall` hook runs `install.js`, which downloads a platform-specific binary from `https://laogou.us/download/veteran/v1.0.2/veteran_<ver>_<platform>_<arch>.{tar.gz,zip}`, writes it to disk, chmods it 0755, and executes it via `execSync` (`"${BIN_PATH}" version`). The header comment in install.js claims the binary is fetched from 'GitHub Releases' and declares `REPO = 'veteran-cli/veteran'`, but the actual `DOWNLOAD_BASE_URL` points to `laogou.us`, which does not match the package's declared homepage (github.com/veteran-cli/veteran). The download version is hardcoded to `v1.0.2` and is decoupled from the package version (1.0.11), so the author can substitute the binary contents server-side at any time. No checksum or signature verification is performed. The combination — mutable third-party host, publisher mismatch, cover-story comment claiming GitHub Releases, no integrity check, fetched bytes immediately executed at install time — is the dropper fingerprint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
